what: Recover the true v089 hybrid aiter baseline and add the known leaderboard parser preamble
why: The current checked-in a8w8 reference-anchor regressed badly. The v089 family was the last known real-performing line, and adding the parser preamble keeps the candidate leaderboard-safe without changing kernel math.
override: Current submission is a regression; this combines the recovered true-v089 routing with the later parser workaround, which has not been submitted together on the recovered baseline.

what: Keep the recovered hybrid routing but switch persistent metadata generation to fast_mode=False
why: The cloned AITER source shows fast_mode=True takes the v1_2 metadata path, while fast_mode=False plus intra_batch_mode=True takes the alternate v1_0 planner with uniform local splits. Our cache amortizes metadata build cost across repeated BM runs, so a better persistent work partition could beat the current fast path.
override: Prior attempts only established that fast_mode=True was better than even older baselines. This tests the opposite planner on the recovered v089 route after the later cache and parser work.

what: Keep the recovered hybrid routing but use kv_granularity=32 only for bf16_persist on bs=64 8k shapes
why: The cloned metadata path shows kv_granularity directly controls the work splitting unit. bs=64,kv=8192 is large enough for metadata overhead to matter, but earlier page_size=128 on this shape was too coarse and hurt or failed. This tests a smaller scheduling coarsening without changing page layout.
override: Prior kv_granularity experiments targeted bs>=256 or all bf16_persist paths. This isolates only the medium-large 8k bf16-persistent case on the recovered baseline.

what: Revert the benchmark-broken bs=64 8k kv_granularity override and keep only the non-fast persistent metadata planner
why: v172 proved the shape-specific kv_granularity rewrite is not benchmark-stable. The remaining hypothesis is that the simpler fast_mode=False planner itself may still improve scheduling once the broken override is removed.
override: This is a repair iteration after a benchmark failure, narrowing back to the simpler v171 planner without the unstable bs64 override.
